description_long: This is a round, rustic loaf of bread with a golden-brown crust and a distinctive crosshatch scoring pattern on its surface. The crust appears thick and slightly flour-dusted, giving it a traditional artisanal look. The loaf is domed, with a gentle rise at the center and a flatter base, suggesting it was baked free-form rather than in a pan. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible cracks and crevices from the scoring. The coloration ranges from light tan to deeper golden hues, especially along the ridges of the scored pattern. There are no visible labels, packaging, or branding on the bread.
description: A round, rustic loaf of bread with a golden-brown, crosshatched crust and a flour-dusted, artisanal appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the loaf from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the crosshatch scoring and the domed top.
description_view_1: The loaf appears flatter from this angle, with the scoring pattern less pronounced and the base more visible.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the rounded edge and the depth of the scoring lines on the crust.
description_view_3: A top-down view clearly displays the symmetrical crosshatch pattern and the even flour dusting.
attribute_crust_pattern: The crust features a prominent crosshatch scoring pattern, with each section lightly dusted with flour. The surface is rough and rustic, with visible cracks and a golden-brown coloration.
